name: French Polynesia Zika–Guillain-Barré case-control study
time_zero_definition: Onset of Guillain-Barré syndrome (cases); preceding transient illness compatible with Zika ~6 days earlier
notes: Case-control design (42 GBS cases; two control groups of 98 age/sex/island-matched patients and 70 Zika-positive patients without neurological signs). Establishes the causal link between Zika infection and an acute post-infectious immune sequela (GBS) rather than a chronic fatigue/pain syndrome; included to span the immune-neurological end of the PAIS spectrum. No longitudinal follow-up of chronic outcomes in this report.

publications.title: Guillain-Barré Syndrome outbreak associated with Zika virus infection in French Polynesia: a case-control study
publications.authors: Cao-Lormeau VM, Blake A, Mons S, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: Guillain-Barré syndrome patients with serological evidence of recent Zika virus infection (anti-ZIKV IgM or IgG)
findings.source_locator: Results (41/42 GBS cases had anti-ZIKV IgM or IgG)
